description_long: This is a large, two-story suburban house with a complex, multi-hipped roof covered in dark shingles. The front facade features a prominent covered entryway with two supporting columns, a small balcony above, and several large windows arranged symmetrically. The exterior walls are finished in a dark green color with lighter trim, and the lower portion of the house is clad in a gray foundation. On one side, there is an attached garage with a sloped roof and a balcony above it, while the opposite side features a partially covered patio area. The rear of the house has additional windows and a secondary balcony, providing ample natural light and outdoor space. The overall design blends traditional and modern suburban architectural elements, making it suitable for a residential neighborhood.
description: A large, two-story suburban house with a dark multi-hipped roof, green exterior walls, prominent entryway, balconies, and an attached garage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with columns, a balcony above, large windows, and the attached garage on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the roof structure, small windows, and the green exterior wall with minimal architectural features.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a secondary balcony, additional windows, and a partially covered patio area on the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the garage roof, fenced patio, and the continuation of the green and gray exterior.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with large picture windows and balcony doors
attribute_entryway_design: Covered entryway with two columns, small balcony above, steps, and a recessed main door
